Monday Night Lights

On Monday evening, I did something slightly unusual: I left the office earl=
y and headed to the Meadowlands to watch my New York Jets take on the Bills=
=2E I=E2=80=99ve been a Jets fan since chi=
ldhood, a bizarre and unforced affl=
iction in an otherwise happy life, and it=E2=80=99s an affinity that I=E2=
=80=99ve regrettably handed down to my children. I=E2=80=99d been looking f=
orward to the game all weekend: Not only had the Jets recently replaced the=
ir head coach, adding an element of intrigue to the game, but I also love t=
he pomp and theater of live sports, especially professional football. Since=
 it was Monday Night Football, ESPN would be dispatching its full team and =
all the agents would be in town to talk business, dap up celebrities, and s=
cour for information. In other words, it seemed like it was going to be an =
evening brimming with sports and gossip=E2=80=94two of my
favorite pastimes.

I was attending the game with two iconic veterans of the industry: Connor S=
chell, the utterly brilliant founder of Words + Pictures and the former hea=
d of content at ESPN, who co-created 30 for 30 and executive produced both =
O.J.: Made in America and The Last Dance, perhaps the two most profoundly i=
mpactful documentaries of the past decade; and Peter Schrager, the NFL Netw=
ork morning show host and Fox NFL analyst, who happens to be this generatio=
n=E2=80=99s heir to Peter King. Indeed, attending an NFL game with Schrager=
, in particular, is sort of like riding through Vatican City in the Popemob=
ile: Everyone came up to him on the sidelines before the game to pay their =
respects. At some point, even Fireman Ed, the team=E2=80=99s vociferous and=
 Jumbotron-ready super fan, kissed the ring=E2=80=94even as J.B. Smoove and=
 50 Cent, among others, milled about.

But my favorite moment of the evening
didn=E2=80=99t even occur between the white lines=E2=80=94the Jets blew it =
in the fourth quarter, inevitably=E2=80=94but rather during the pregame. As=
 we were milling about on the sidelines, shortly after 7 p.m., I noticed a =
number of agents, P.R. executives, and entourage members stare down at thei=
r phones, one by one, like a chorus line. I was unsure precisely what they =
were looking at until I heard one of them mumble, Ourand is out. Indeed, th=
e latest issue of The Varsity ( https://puck.news/newsletters/the-varsity/?=
utm_campaign=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%2810%2F19%2F24%29&utm_content=
=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%2810%2F19%2F24%29&utm_medium=3Demail_action=
&utm_source=3Dcustomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd12a ), my partner John =
Ourand=E2=80=99s brilliant private email on the sports media business, had =
popped into their inboxes only moments earlier and they were reading it lik=
e doorbusters at Walmart on Black Friday. I even had the funny pleasure of =
standing beside CAA Sports agent Matt Kramer as he
read
a piece about himself. It was both meta and heady=E2=80=94a true testament =
to John=E2=80=99s singular influence in the industry, and what he has creat=
ed here at Puck.

Coincidentally, only a few days earlier, the ESPN chairman Jimmy Pitaro had=
 quietly come to an agreement with the NFL to co-broadcast six additional M=
onday Night Football games on ABC, its sister broadcast network, starting w=
ith that very Jets-Bills contest. And while that might not seem like an eno=
rmous concession, it was actually quite a powder keg in the ever-metamorpho=
sing media world. Not only was Disney, the parent company, foregoing ESPN=
=E2=80=99s cable exclusivity, but this was naturally going to enrage the NF=
L=E2=80=99s other broadcast partners. After all, NBC markets its enormously=
 valuable Sunday night package as the only primetime broadcast deal. A few =
days earlier, NFL Media C.O.O. Hans Schroeder had broken the ABC simulcast =
news to executives at NBC and Fox and CBS, all of whom
manifested emotions on the outer edge of the rage spectrum.

If you only have time to read one story this weekend, I=E2=80=99d urge you =
to turn your attention to John=E2=80=99s excellent piece, Is Goodell Pickin=
g Favorites? ( https://puck.news/espn-abc-monday-night-football-simulcast-h=
as-rivals-networks-hot/?utm_campaign=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%2810%2F=
19%2F24%29&utm_content=3DThe+Backstory+-+SUBSCRIBERS+%2810%2F19%2F24%29&utm=
_medium=3Demail_action&utm_source=3Dcustomer.io&utm_term=3Df6c60617da42abd1=
2a ) Even if you have little interest in sports, or even the business of sp=
ort, the story articulates how deeply the future of the world=E2=80=99s lar=
gest media conglomerates relies on their relationship with the NFL, one of =
the last ballasts of American monoculture. Indeed, this latest ESPN-ABC sim=
ulcast deal perfectly represented The Shield=E2=80=99s power. The league wa=
nted to increase the viewership for Monday Night Football, so it broadcast =
the game
on a second network. And it did so because it could. These days, after all,=
 CBS owner Paramount Global has a market capitalization of around $7.5 bill=
ion, which is just a little more than Apollo co-founder Josh Harris paid fo=
r the Commanders a year ago, and probably around what the Seahawks will fet=
ch when Paul Allen=E2=80=99s sister puts the team on the market=E2=80=94per=
haps enticing Jeff Bezos. Indeed, this is one of the great dynamics of our =
time, and precisely what you should expect from Puck.

          <td style=3D"padding-bottom: 36px; color: #000000; font-size: 16p=
x; line-height: 30px; margin-top: 0; margin-bottom: 0;">On Monday evening, =
I did something slightly unusual: I left the office early and headed to the=
 Meadowlands to watch my New York Jets take on the Bills. I=E2=80=99ve been=
 a Jets fan since childhood, a bizarre and unforced affliction in an otherw=
ise happy life, and it=E2=80=99s an affinity that I=E2=80=99ve regrettably =
handed down to my children. I=E2=80=99d been looking forward to the game al=
l weekend: Not only had the Jets recently replaced their head coach, adding=
 an element of intrigue to the game, but I also love the pomp and theater o=
f live sports, especially professional football. Since it was <i>Monday Nig=
ht Football</i>, ESPN would be dispatching its full team and all the agents=
 would be in town to talk business, dap up celebrities, and scour for infor=
mation. In other words, it seemed like it was going to be an evening brimmi=
ng with sports and gossip=E2=80=94two of my
favorite pastimes.<br/><br/>I was attending the game with two iconic vetera=
ns of the industry: <b>Connor Schell</b>, the utterly brilliant founder of =
Words + Pictures and the former head of content at ESPN, who co-created <i>=
30 for 30 </i>and executive produced both <i>O.J.: Made in America </i>and =
<i>The Last Dance</i>, perhaps the two most profoundly impactful documentar=
ies of the past decade; and <b>Peter Schrager</b>, the NFL Network morning =
show host and Fox NFL analyst, who happens to be this generation=E2=80=99s =
heir to <b>Peter King</b>. Indeed, attending an NFL game with Schrager, in =
particular, is sort of like riding through Vatican City in the Popemobile: =
<i>Everyone </i>came up to him on the sidelines before the game to pay thei=
r respects. At some point, even <b>Fireman Ed</b>, the team=E2=80=99s vocif=
erous and Jumbotron-ready super fan, kissed the ring=E2=80=94even as <b>J.B=
=2E Smoove </b>and <b>50 Cent</b>, amon=
g others, milled about.<br/><br/>But m=
y favorite moment of the evening
didn=E2=80=99t even occur between the white lines=E2=80=94the Jets blew it =
in the fourth quarter, inevitably=E2=80=94but rather during the pregame. As=
 we were milling about on the sidelines, shortly after 7 p.m., I noticed a =
number of agents, P.R. executives, and entourage members stare down at thei=
r phones, one by one, like a chorus line. I was unsure precisely what they =
were looking at until I heard one of them mumble, <i>Ourand is out</i>. Ind=
eed, the latest issue of <u><a href=3D"https://email.puck.news/e/c/eyJlbWFp=
bF9pZCI6ImRnVDJ4Z1lEQUt2UktxclJLZ0dTcFFaWEhzUC13UXMzaHhiVE40WT0iLCJocmVmIjo=
iaHR0cHM6Ly9wdWNrLm5ld3MvbmV3c2xldHRlcnMvdGhlLXZhcnNpdHkvP3V0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj=
1UaGUrQmFja3N0b3J5Ky0rU1VCU0NSSUJFUlMrJTI4MTAlMkYxOSUyRjI0JTI5XHUwMDI2dXRtX=
2NvbnRlbnQ9VGhlK0JhY2tzdG9yeSstK1NVQlNDUklCRVJTKyUyODEwJTJGMTklMkYyNCUyOVx1=
MDAyNnV0bV9tZWRpdW09ZW1haWxfYWN0aW9uXHUwMDI2dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1jdXN0b21lci5pb1x=
1MDAyNnV0bV90ZXJtPWY2YzYwNjE3ZGE0MmFiZDEyYSIsImludGVybmFsIjoiZjZjNjA2MTdkYT=
QyYWJkMTJhIiwibGlua19pZCI6NzU0ODY0fQ/b876acba2e24645d449074bea620e61e4e4fe1=
82c80a1482eef941d366554420" style=3D"color:#d40000;text-decoration:none;fon=
t-weight:bold" class=3D"sdf">The Varsity</a></u>, my partner <b>John Ourand=
</b>=E2=80=99s brilliant private email on the sports media business, had po=
pped into their inboxes only moments earlier and they were reading it like =
doorbusters at Walmart on Black Friday. I even had the funny pleasure of st=
anding beside CAA Sports agent <b>Matt Kramer </b>as he read
a piece about himself. It was both meta and heady=E2=80=94a true testament =
to John=E2=80=99s singular influence in the industry, and what he has creat=
ed here at Puck.<br/><br/>Coincidentally, only a few days earlier, the ESPN=
 chairman <b>Jimmy Pitaro </b>had quietly come to an agreement with the NFL=
 to co-broadcast six additional <i>Monday Night Football </i>games on ABC, =
its sister broadcast network, starting with that very Jets-Bills contest. A=
nd while that might not seem like an enormous concession, it was actually q=
uite a powder keg in the ever-metamorphosing media world. Not only was Disn=
ey, the parent company, foregoing ESPN=E2=80=99s cable exclusivity, but thi=
s was naturally going to enrage the NFL=E2=80=99s other <i>broadcast </i>pa=
rtners. After all, NBC markets its enormously valuable Sunday night package=
 as the only primetime broadcast deal. A few days earlier, NFL Media C.O.O.=
 <b>Hans Schroeder </b>had broken the ABC simulcast news to executives at N=
BC and Fox and CBS, all of whom
manifested emotions on the outer edge of the rage spectrum.<br/><br/>If you=
 only have time to read one story this weekend, I=E2=80=99d urge you to tur=
n your attention to John=E2=80=99s excellent piece, <i><u><a href=3D"https:=
//email.puck.news/e/c/eyJlbWFpbF9pZCI6ImRnVDJ4Z1lEQUt2UktxclJLZ0dTcFFaWEhzU=
C13UXMzaHhiVE40WT0iLCJocmVmIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9wdWNrLm5ld3MvZXNwbi1hYmMtbW9uZGF5=
LW5pZ2h0LWZvb3RiYWxsLXNpbXVsY2FzdC1oYXMtcml2YWxzLW5ldHdvcmtzLWhvdC8_dXRtX2N=
hbXBhaWduPVRoZStCYWNrc3RvcnkrLStTVUJTQ1JJQkVSUyslMjgxMCUyRjE5JTJGMjQlMjlcdT=
AwMjZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1UaGUrQmFja3N0b3J5Ky0rU1VCU0NSSUJFUlMrJTI4MTAlMkYxOSUyR=
jI0JTI5XHUwMDI2dXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbF9hY3Rpb25cdTAwMjZ1dG1fc291cmNlPWN1c3Rv=
bWVyLmlvXHUwMDI2dXRtX3Rlcm09ZjZjNjA2MTdkYTQyYWJkMTJhIiwiaW50ZXJuYWwiOiJmNmM=
2MDYxN2RhNDJhYmQxMmEiLCJsaW5rX2lkIjoxMTQwNDgzfQ/bd74f391a72122574a717979b3f=
62b4b52b22200106ba234909d29d39cdf797c" style=3D"color:#d40000;text-decorati=
on:none;font-weight:bold" class=3D"sdf">Is Goodell Picking Favorites?</a></=
u></i> Even if you have little interest in sports, or even the business of =
sport, the story articulates how deeply the future of the world=E2=80=99s l=
argest media conglomerates relies on their relationship with the NFL, one o=
f the last ballasts of American monoculture. Indeed, this latest ESPN-ABC s=
imulcast deal perfectly represented The Shield=E2=80=99s power. The league =
wanted to increase the viewership for <i>Monday Night Football</i>, so it b=
roadcast the game
on a second network. And it did so because it <i>could</i>. These days, aft=
er all, CBS owner Paramount Global has a market capitalization of around $7=
=2E5 billion, which is just a little mo=
re than Apollo co-founder <b>Josh Harr=
is </b>paid for the Commanders a year ago, and probably around what the Sea=
hawks will fetch when <b>Paul Allen</b>=E2=80=99s sister puts the team on t=
he market=E2=80=94perhaps enticing <b>Jeff Bezos</b>. Indeed, this is one o=
f the great dynamics of our time, and precisely what you should expect from=
 Puck.<br/><br/>Have a great weekend,<br/>Jon<br/></td>
